items.title: CMS issues guidance on new limits for federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens (effective Oct. 1, 2026)
items.summary: CMS released operational guidance for states on implementing a statutory change that limits claiming federal matching funds in Medicaid and CHIP for individuals who are not U.S. citizens or U.S. nationals (or who fall into specified noncitizen categories). The change takes effect October 1, 2026, with exceptions that still allow federal matching funds for emergency medical conditions and for optional coverage of lawfully residing children and pregnant women.

items.title: KFF: Medicaid work requirements for ACA expansion adults required starting Jan. 1, 2027 (states may start earlier)
items.summary: KFF reports that a 2025 reconciliation law requires states to condition Medicaid eligibility for adults in the ACA expansion group (and partial expansion waiver programs in Georgia and Wisconsin) on meeting work requirements starting January 1, 2027. KFF notes states have the option to implement requirements earlier, and implementation will require new eligibility information collection, systems changes, and outreach.

items.title: CBPP: SNAP participation fell 2.5 million (6%) between July 2025 enactment of H.R. 1 and December 2025 USDA data
items.summary: CBPP’s SNAP Tracker cites USDA data showing nationwide SNAP participation fell by 2.5 million people (6%) between July 2025 (when H.R. 1 was enacted) and December 2025 (the latest month of USDA data). CBPP notes some states saw much larger declines (for example Arizona’s reported 32% decline, and 47% using more recent state data), and links the changes to the implementation of H.R. 1 policies.
items.source: Center on Budget and Policy Priorities

items.title: HUD proposes optional work requirements and term limits for certain assisted households; public comments due May 1, 2026
items.summary: A HUD proposed rule in the Federal Register would amend HUD regulations to allow Public Housing Agencies (PHAs) and certain multifamily owners to choose to implement work requirements for work-eligible adults and term limits for non-elderly, non-disabled families in covered HUD rental assistance programs. The public comment period is open through May 1, 2026.

items.title: HHS proposes reinstating LIHEAP Household Report information collection; public comments due June 8, 2026
items.summary: HHS’s Office of Community Services (OCS) published a Federal Register notice to reinstate the LIHEAP Household Report (OMB Control No. 0970-0060), which states use to report households assisted with LIHEAP block grant funds. The notice sets a public comment deadline of June 8, 2026.

items.title: IRS: Anyone can file a free federal tax filing extension through IRS Free File (pay by April 15; file by Oct. 15)
items.summary: The IRS says anyone can use an IRS Free File partner to file an extension, even if they do not qualify for Free File for preparing a return. The extension gives taxpayers until October 15 to file, but taxpayers still need to file the extension and pay any balance due by April 15 to avoid penalties.

items.title: USDA WIC memo: states must authorize at least one non-fresh fruit/veg form by April 2026; FY26 CVV/B amounts include $48 for pregnant/postpartum
items.summary: USDA FNS says WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026. For FY 2026, FNS reports an 11.8% inflation adjustment over the FY 2022 baseline and lists monthly CVV/B amounts of $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for breastfeeding participants.

items.title: SSA: Social Security and SSI benefits increase 2.8% in 2026 (SSI increases start Dec. 31, 2025)
items.summary: The Social Security Administration announced a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2026, with Social Security benefit increases beginning in January 2026 and SSI increases beginning December 31, 2025. SSA also said the taxable maximum for 2026 is slated to rise to $184,500 (from $176,100).
